clusterConfig.description: Cluster config read from the cluster

createdAt.description: The time when this cluster was registered with Signadot.

name.description: The name of the cluster.

operator.description: Information about the Signadot Operator in this cluster.

createdAt.description: The time when this cluster was registered with Signadot.

lastConnectedAt.description: The last time at which this token was used to connect.

lastConnectedIp.description: The last IP address from which this token connected.

devboxId.description: Required: deterministic ID from user+metadata hash

description.description: Description is an optional human-readable description.

apiKeyMasked.description: APIKeyMasked is set when ActorType is "api_key".

userEmail.description: UserEmail is set when ActorType is "user".

id.description: Deterministic ID: hash of user ID + metadata (for user-owned) or hash of metadata only (for API key-owned, org-scoped)

metadata.description: Metadata (all key-value pairs). Only "name" and "machine-id" are used for ID generation.

user.description: User email attribute (for display/authorization). Empty string for API key-owned devboxes (org-scoped).

metadata.description: Required: metadata key-value pairs. Only "name" and "machine-id" are used for ID generation.

auth.description:
Auth is always present. Principal and OnBehalfOf are rendered as explicit
null (not omitted) when there is no principal — these are not omitempty so
the null is discoverable: principal is null for system actions and older
events whose user is gone; onBehalfOf is reserved (currently always null).

name.description: user email or SA name (display)

completed.description: Completed is the number of steps that finished successfully.

failed.description: Failed is the number of steps that finished with an error.

init.description: Init is the number of steps that have not yet started.

running.description: Running is the number of steps currently executing.

skipped.description: Skipped is the number of steps that were not executed.

waiting.description: Waiting is the number of steps waiting for routing context readiness.

managedBy.description:
ManagedBy indicates whether this action is managed by the system
(synced from git) or by an org (created via API).

body.description:
Body is the action implementation as markdown with code blocks.
Inputs and outputs are declared inline via \input{...} and \output{...} syntax.

published.description: Published controls whether this action is visible and usable.

bodyImage.description: BodyImage is the image directive parsed from \image{...} in the body, if any.

bodyOutputs.description: BodyOutputs are the output fields parsed from \output{...} directives in the body.

bodyParams.description: BodyParams are the input parameters parsed from \input{...} directives in the body.

createdAt.description: CreatedAt is when the action was created. Serialized as RFC3339.

description.description: Description is a short one-line description parsed from the \description{...} directive in the body.

enabled.description:
Enabled is the org's effective view on whether this action can be used.
Resolved from per-org overrides and system defaults via the same
precedence the compile path applies. Distinct from Spec.Published,
which is the action author's intent.

extraInputsSchemaPolicy.description:
ExtraInputsSchemaPolicy is parsed from the \extra_inputs_schema{...}
directive in the body. nil when the action did not declare one.

updatedAt.description: UpdatedAt is when the action was last updated. Serialized as RFC3339.

refs.description: Refs maps param names to reference expressions (e.g. "params.cluster", "steps.foo.outputs.bar").

values.description: Values maps param names to literal JSON values.

checksum.description: Checksum is the base64-encoded SHA-256 digest.

error.description: Error is set if the artifact upload failed.

ready.description: Ready is true once the artifact has been uploaded to S3.

size.description: Size is the artifact size in bytes.

fromAnyTarget.description:
FromAnyTarget names a plan param whose value is a RoutingTarget.
The cluster is inferred from the variant provided at execution time
(sandbox → sandbox cluster, routeGroup → RG cluster).

fromCluster.description: FromCluster names a plan param whose value is a cluster name directly.

fromRouteGroup.description:
FromRouteGroup names a plan param whose value is a route group name.
If the route group is bound to a single cluster, it resolves automatically.
If the route group spans multiple clusters, the execution must specify
a cluster (validated against the route group's cluster set).

fromSandbox.description:
FromSandbox names a plan param whose value is a sandbox name.
The cluster is inferred from the sandbox's single cluster.

pattern.description:
Pattern is a glob pattern matched against cluster names (e.g. "prod-*",
"*"). If the pattern matches exactly one connected cluster with an
available runner, it is used automatically. Otherwise, the execution
must specify a cluster.

prompt.description: Prompt is the markdown source to compile into a Plan.

selectionHint.description:
SelectionHint, if set, is written onto the resulting plan's
PlanSpec.SelectionHint field (the agent-readable "when to pick
this plan" hint surfaced on tag-list responses). Optional;
preserved across `plan recompile`.

status.description: Status is the runtime execution state.
Status is the runtime execution state.

cluster.description: Cluster is the target cluster for this execution.

params.description: Params are the actual values bound to the plan's declared parameters.

planID.description: PlanID is the ID of the compiled plan being executed.

runner.description: Runner is the runner group name assigned to execute the plan.

secrets.description:
Secrets maps plan param names to org secret names. At dispatch time,
the control plane resolves each secret name to its decrypted value and
delivers it to the runner alongside regular params.
API responses return the name mapping (never decrypted values).

completedAt.description: CompletedAt is when the execution finished. Serialized as RFC3339.

createdAt.description: CreatedAt is when the execution was created. Serialized as RFC3339.

error.description:
Error is a human-readable error message for execution-level failures
(e.g. artifact upload failures, plan output resolution errors).
Step-level errors are in StepStatus.Error.

inputs.description:
Inputs records how each plan-level input was resolved at dispatch
time. Populated at the transition to dispatching. Mirrors Outputs.

outputs.description: Outputs contains the resolved plan-level outputs, populated on completion.

phase.description: Phase is the overall execution state.

runnerHost.description: RunnerHost is the hostname of the runner executing the plan.

stepCounts.description: StepCounts contains the count of steps in each step phase.

steps.description: Steps contains the per-step runtime status.

updatedAt.description: UpdatedAt is when the execution status was last updated. Serialized as RFC3339.

default.description:
Default is the schema to apply when the plan author omitted one.
Empty (nil) when this primitive is not set.

require.description:
Require, when true, makes plan compile fail if any extra_input on
a step of this action is schemaless.

default.description: Default is the default value used when the field is not provided. Must conform to Schema if set.

metadata.description:
Metadata carries presentation hints and additional key-value pairs.
Well-known keys: "contentType" (MIME type), "displayName" (UI label),
"encoding" (e.g. "base64" for inlined binary values).

name.description: Name is the field identifier, unique within the resource's params or outputs.

required.description: Required indicates whether the field must be provided.

schema.description: Schema is an optional JSON Schema describing the field's type and constraints.

schemaRef.description: SchemaRef is an optional reference to an external schema (name, file, or URL).

env.description:
Env carries env entries with literal values (KEY -> literal).
Applies in both paths. Reserved runner-set keys (SIGNADOT_*, HOME,
TMPDIR, PATH) silently lose to the runner's values on collision.

envFromInput.description:
EnvFromInput carries env entries whose values resolve from a
declared input at run time (KEY -> input name). Applies in both
paths. Same reserved-key filtering as Env. The same KEY may not
appear in both Env and EnvFromInput.

inputName.description:
InputName names a declared input whose value at run time is the
image reference. Set when the action declared
\image{ref={"input":"NAME"}}. If the input resolves to the empty
string, the action runs in the runner's base image.

literal.description:
Literal is the image reference itself, e.g. "alpine:3.19". Set when
the action declared \image{"..."} or \image{ref="..."}. An empty
literal means "run in the runner's base image".

lastReferencedAt.description:
LastReferencedAt is the most recent timestamp at which a plan
execution or visible action referenced this ref, computed at the
apiserver. Zero when neither source supplies a timestamp.

ref.description:
Ref is the canonicalized image reference, e.g.
"index.docker.io/library/alpine:3.19".

referenceCount.description:
ReferenceCount is the number of distinct plan executions and
actions that referenced this ref over the apiserver's window.
Zero when neither source supplies a count.

name.description: Name is the plan param name.

resolvedVia.description:
ResolvedVia is the source that populated the value. Valid values at
plan scope: literal, callerSecret, default, unbound.

secretName.description:
SecretName is the caller-supplied secret name when
ResolvedVia == callerSecret. Empty otherwise.

artifact.description: Artifact is a reference to the log stored in S3.

stream.description: Stream is the log stream type ("stdout" or "stderr").

value.description: Value holds the log content inline if small enough.

artifact.description: Artifact holds a reference to the S3-stored output.

drill.description:
Drill is the path of segments to walk inside the source step
output's parsed JSON to extract the actual plan output value. Set
only for drill plan outputs (e.g. ["response", "statusCode"] for
"steps.send.outputs.capture.response.statusCode"). The drill is
applied at download time by the apiserver — the runner records
it but does not materialize the drilled value, so the source
step's existing artifact (or inline Value) is reused without any
extra upload.

metadata.description: Metadata carries presentation hints (e.g. contentType, displayName).

name.description: Name is the output field name.

stepRef.description:
StepRef identifies the source step output for alias and drill plan
outputs. Set when the plan output is a direct reference to a step
output (steps.X.outputs.Y), regardless of whether the value is
inlined or stored as an artifact, AND for drill outputs
(steps.X.outputs.Y.field) where it identifies the source whose
bytes the apiserver will fetch and walk via Drill.

value.description: Value holds the inlined output value (small text/JSON).

literal.description: Literal is an inline routing target.

ref.description: Ref contains reference expressions that resolve to a routing target.

cluster.description:
Cluster declares the plan's cluster affinity. If nil, the plan is
cluster-agnostic and runs on any cluster with an available runner.

output.description: Output maps output names to reference expressions wiring step outputs to the plan's external interface.

params.description: Params are the external parameters the plan accepts from its caller.

prompt.description:
Prompt is the natural-language prompt body that was compiled to produce this plan.
Empty for hand-created plans.

runner.description:
Runner is the plan author's preferred runner group name.
If set, this runner is used directly at execution time. Conflicts
with an execution-level runner.

selectionHint.description:
SelectionHint is a one-line agent-readable description of when to
pick this plan. Surfaced on tag-list responses so an agent
scanning the catalog of tagged plans can pick by purpose
(analogous to how an action's Description carries "what this
action does"). Free-form prose; aim for one sentence.

steps.description: Steps are the action invocations that make up the plan's DAG.

compiledFrom.description:
CompiledFrom is the planID of the source plan, if this plan was produced
by recompiling an existing plan via POST /plans/{id}/compile.

createdAt.description: CreatedAt is when the plan was created. Serialized as RFC3339.

createdBy.description: CreatedBy describes who created this plan.

stepDependencies.description:
StepDependencies is the step dependency graph, computed from ref expressions.
Keys are step IDs; values are the step IDs that step depends on.

action.description: Action is the inlined action contract for this step.

args.description:
Args provides the refs and literal values bound to the action's params
and extra inputs.

condition.description:
Condition is an expr-lang expression evaluated against the step's resolved
args. Must evaluate to true for the step to execute.

extraInputs.description:
ExtraInputs declares additional named inputs for this step beyond
the action's declared params. These are wired via Args.Refs like
regular params and become part of the action's eval environment.

extraOutputs.description:
ExtraOutputs declares additional named outputs for this step beyond
the action's declared outputs. The action writes these to ./outputs/<name>
and the runner reads them back according to their schema declarations.
Names must not shadow the action's declared outputs.

id.description: ID is the unique identifier for this step within the plan.

routingContext.description: RoutingContext specifies the sandbox or route group this step targets.

actionID.description:
ActionID is the action's ID. The only field a hand-authored plan
must set on a step's action; the server hydrates the rest.

body.description: Body is the action implementation (markdown with code blocks).

extraInputsSchemaPolicy.description:
ExtraInputsSchemaPolicy declares how missing schemas on this
step's extra_inputs are resolved. Nil when the action did not
declare an \extra_inputs_schema{...} directive.

image.description:
Image, if set, declares that the action's shell fenced block runs
inside an OCI image via actionbox run-image. Either Literal or
InputName is set; never both.

name.description:
Name is the action's name at the time the snapshot was taken.
Display label, not the canonical identifier — ActionID is. At
create time the snapshot must round-trip the registry (an
explicitly supplied Name that doesn't match is rejected); at
execute time the snapshot is honored as-is so renames in the
registry don't break stored plans. Filled from the registry when
omitted on input; otherwise preserved as supplied.

outputs.description: Outputs are the output fields parsed from the action's body.

params.description: Params are the input parameters parsed from the action's body.

revision.description:
Revision is the registered action revision the snapshot was taken
from. Optional on input — set to pin the step to a historical
revision; otherwise the action's current revision is recorded.

timeout.description:
Timeout is a literal wall-clock duration string (parsed via
time.ParseDuration, e.g. "60s", "5m") parsed from the top-level
\timeout{"..."} directive. Empty means the system default cap
applies. Mutually exclusive with TimeoutInputName.

timeoutInputName.description:
TimeoutInputName names a declared input whose value at run time is
the duration string. Set when the action declared
\timeout{{"input":"NAME"}}. Mutually exclusive with Timeout.

name.description: Name is the step input name (action param or extra input).

ref.description:
Ref is the ref expression text when ResolvedVia == ref
(e.g. "params.region", "steps.s1.outputs.token").

resolvedVia.description:
ResolvedVia is the source that populated the value. Valid values at
step scope: literal, ref, default, unbound.

artifact.description: Artifact holds a reference to the S3-stored output.

metadata.description: Metadata carries presentation hints (e.g. contentType, displayName).

name.description: Name is the output field name.

value.description: Value holds the inlined output value (small text/JSON).

error.description: Error is a human-readable error message, populated when Phase is failed.

id.description: ID is the step identifier, matching the corresponding Step.ID in the spec.

inputs.description:
Inputs records how each of this step's declared inputs was resolved.
Populated at the transition to running. Mirrors Outputs.

logs.description:
Logs contains the step's captured log streams (stdout, stderr),
populated when the step reaches a terminal phase.

outputs.description: Outputs contains the step's output statuses, populated when Phase is completed.

phase.description: Phase is the current execution state of the step.

name.description: Name is the unique tag name within the org.

plan.description: Plan is the resolved plan this tag points to. Populated in responses.

planID.description: PlanID is the ID of the plan this tag points to.

createdAt.description: CreatedAt is when the tag was created. Serialized as RFC3339.

updatedAt.description: UpdatedAt is when the tag was last updated. Serialized as RFC3339.

cluster.description: Cluster that the resource is created in

name.description: Name of the created resource

sandbox.description: Name of the sandbox containing the resource

createdAt.description: The time at which the resource plugin was created

name.description:
Plugin identity of the form `bareName[@semver]`. The bare-name
form (no `@`) means the default version (0.0.0); responses
for plugins at the default version use this bare form for
backward compatibility with pre-versioning clients.

updatedAt.description: The time at which the resource plugin was last updated

routeGroup.description: RouteGroup is the name of the target route group.

routingKey.description: RoutingKey is the routing key used to direct traffic.

sandbox.description: Sandbox is the name of the target sandbox.

anyRef.description:
AnyRef is a reference to a plan param whose value is a RoutingTarget
(polymorphic — the caller decides sandbox, routeGroup, or routingKey).

routeGroupRef.description: RouteGroupRef is a reference expression that resolves to a route group name.

routingKeyRef.description: RoutingKeyRef is a reference expression that resolves to a routing key.

sandboxRef.description: SandboxRef is a reference expression that resolves to a sandbox name.

name.description: Human-readable name of this sandbox

createdAt.description: CreatedAt is when the secret was created. Read-only.

description.description: Description is an optional human-readable description.

name.description: Name is the unique identifier within the org.

updatedAt.description: UpdatedAt is when the secret was last updated. Read-only.

value.description:
Value is the secret plaintext. Write-only: accepted on create/update,
never returned in any API response.

createdAt.description: CreatedAt is when the service account was created. Read-only.

description.description: Description is an optional human-readable description.

name.description: Name is the unique identifier within the org.

role.description:
Role is the authorization role for the service account: "admin" or
"member".

updatedAt.description: UpdatedAt is when the service account was last updated. Read-only.

planID.description: PlanID is the plan this tag pointed to during this period.

taggedAt.description: TaggedAt is when the tag was assigned to this plan. Serialized as RFC3339.

untaggedAt.description: UntaggedAt is when the tag was moved or deleted. Empty if current.

enabled.description: Enabled = true, requires the definition of a valid routing context

publish.description:
Publish represents whether the test execution will show up by default
in the UI, i.e. to filter out ad-hoc and test the test usage.

timeout.description:
Timeout represents an optional timeout for the test execution.
If not supplied, it defaults to the DefaultTimeout of the associated
test, if that is present.  If that is not present, it defaults to "5m".
It should be a string representing an unsigned integer not exceeding 32 bits followed by
a units character, which can be one of the following.
  - 'm' for minutes
  - 'h' for hours
  - 'd' for days
  - 'w' for weeks

hosted.description: Hosted/External source information, these are one or the other

triggeredBy.description: info about the trigger of the execution, empty means a manual trigger

publish.description:
Publish represents whether the test execution will show up by default
in the UI, i.e. to filter out ad-hoc and test the test usage.

timeout.description:
Timeout represents an optional timeout for the test execution.
If not supplied, it defaults to the DefaultTimeout of the associated
test, if that is present.  If that is not present, it defaults to "5m".
It should be a string representing an unsigned integer not exceeding 32 bits followed by
a units character, which can be one of the following.
  - 'm' for minutes
  - 'h' for hours
  - 'd' for days
  - 'w' for weeks

defaultTimeout.description:
DefaultTimeout represents an optional default timeout for the test
executions associated with this test, which can override this value.
If not supplied, it defaults to "5m".  It should be a string
representing an unsigned integer in decimal not exceeding 32 bits
followed by a units character, which can be one of the following.
  - 'm' for minutes
  - 'h' for hours
  - 'd' for days
  - 'w' for weeks

executionTemplate.description: ExecutionTemplate specifies a template for creating the actual execution

sandboxOf.description:
SandboxOf specifies a workload that when being sandboxed, will
trigger an execution of the test (for now, only forks are supported)

version.description:
The operator version of the cluster.  This may be
nil if the cluster has not yet connected to Signadot.

proxy.description: control plane proxy can be "enabled" or "disabled"

enabled.description: Explicitly enable traffic manager in runner pods for this job.

injectRoutingKey.description:
InjectRoutingKey indicates if the system should perform automatic
injection of routing key headers or not.  It may be the empty string,
"auto" or "disabled".  The empty string defaults to meaning "disabled".
The "auto" value will overwrite the value of the Enabled field, setting
it to true.

jobTimeout.description:
JobTimeout specifies the max job execution time of jobs running with this
RunnerGroup.  If not specified, it defaults to 30 minutes ("30m")

reusePods.description:
ReusePods specifies whether JRG pods are reused after job executions.
If false (default), JRG pods are always recreated after each job.

name.description: Name of the entity within the Kubernetes cluster.

namespace.description: Namespace within which the entity lives in the Kubernetes cluster.

clusterID.description: ID of Authenticated Cluster, if any (via signadot-cluster-token header)

featureFlags.description: Feature Flags as result of authentication

userFlags.description: Flags of authenticated user, if any

size.description: Size is the requested PVC capacity, e.g. "10Gi".

storageClass.description:
StorageClass is the Kubernetes storage class name. Empty string
uses the cluster default.

namespace.description: Namespace to create this runner instance in

create.description: Create refers to the `create` steps for spinning up the resource

delete.description: Delete refers to the `delete` steps for spinning up the resource

resources.description: Resources created using the resource plugin

name.description: Name for the step

script.description: Script to execute in the step

as.description:
If the As field is not speficied, we will take the input name
uppercase it, convert "-" to "_", and inject it as an env var into
the main container

name.description: Name for the input

valueFromSandbox.description: ValueFromSandbox defines whether or not to source value from the sandbox spec

valueFromStep.description:
ValueFromStep is an optional string specifying the output of
another step when not nil.  Exactly one of ValueFromSandbox
and 'ValueFromStep != nil' must hold.

env.description:
Inject the input as an env variable with name Env inside the
main container

path.description: Expose the input as a file at Path inside the main container

name.description: Name of the output variable

valueFromPath.description: Path within the main container from where the output will be read

name.description: Name of the step

output.description: Name of the variable from the step

all.description:
When All is non-nil, T matches a set of labels if and only if every element of All matches them.
Only one field may be non-nil.

any.description:
When Any is non-nil, T matches a set of labels if and only if some element of Any matches them.
Only one field may be non-nil.

label.description:
When Label is non-nil, T matches a set of labels L if and only if t.Label.Matches(L).
Only one field may be non-nil.

key.description:
Key is an exact match on a label key, glob
characters are just considered normal characters.

value.description:
Value is a glob match for the value of a
sandbox label.  See https://pkg.go.dev/path/filepath#Match
for the file glob syntax.

cluster.description:
Cluster gives the cluster to which the route group applies.
If left empty, this RG will become a multi-cluster RG..

description.description: Description provides a short description of the route group.

endpoints.description:
Endpoints define endpoints which target different in-cluster
services.

match.description:
Match defines a matcher for labels, which matches
sandboxes that should be a part of this route group.

ttl.description: TTL define when/how the route group should be deleted

matchedSandboxes.description:
MatchedSandboxes gives the list of sandboxes whose labels
were matched by the 'match' object in the spec.

message.description:
Message is a human readable explanation of why
the sandbox is healthy or not.

ready.description:
Ready indicates whether the sandbox is ready,
meaning that it can be used for testing.

reason.description:
Reason is a machine readable explanation of why
the sandbox is healthy or not.

scheduledDeleteTime.description:
ScheduledDeleteTime returns the formatted and computed ttl based on the Duration
and OffsetFrom

duration.description:
Duration represents the duration until routegroup's end of life.
It should be an unsigned integer not exceeding 32 bits followed by
a units character, which can be one of the following.
  - 'm' for minutes
  - 'h' for hours
  - 'd' for days
  - 'w' for weeks

offsetFrom.description:
OffsetFrom indicates what the Duration is relative to.  It
may be the empty string, "noMatchedSandboxes", "createdAt" or "updatedAt". The empty string
defaults to meaning "noMatchedSandboxes".

devbox.description: Machine to which local mappings in this sandbox may be linked.

session.description: signadot local connect session id and info.

env.description: Environment variable modifications that will be applied to the forked workload

images.description: One or more docker images that will be applied to the forked workload

container.description: name of container to which it applies

name.description: environmental variable name

customizations.description: Customizations specify the customizations we will apply on the fork

forkOf.description:
ForkOf specifies the target workload that we will be forking.  In this usage
the Kind of the ForkOf object must not be a service.

name.description: Name of the endpoint

port.description: Port it will map to on the forked workload

name.description: Name of the entity within the Kubernetes cluster.

namespace.description: Namespace within which the entity lives in the Kubernetes cluster.

host.description: Hostname that this endpoint points to

name.description: Name of the endpoint

port.description: Port it will map to on the specified host

container.description: Container is the container this image must apply to in the forked workload

connection.description: Connection describes an associated call to signadot local connect.

jobs.description: status of the jobs running in the context of the given sandbox

message.description:
Message is a human readable explanation of why
the sandbox is healthy or not.

ready.description:
Ready indicates whether the sandbox is ready,
meaning that it can be used for testing.

reason.description:
Reason is a machine readable explanation of why
the sandbox is healthy or not.

scheduledDeleteTime.description:
ScheduledDeleteTime returns the formatted and computed ttl based on the Duration
and OffsetFrom

testExecutions.description: status of the test executions running in the context of the given sandbox

name.description: Unique name for this resource (used to reference it in env vars and files)

plugin.description: Plugin name, optionally suffixed with @version (e.g. my-plugin@1.2.0)

cluster.description: Cluster within which this sandbox should be created

connection.description: What signadot local connect connection to use.

defaultRouteGroup.description: DefaultRouteGroup can be used to define route group endpoints for the sandbox.

description.description: Description of the purpose of this sandbox

disableSandboxTrafficManager.description:
DisableSandboxTrafficManager provides a way of turning off the sandbox
traffic manager for forks in this sandbox.
Deprecated in operators since v1.1. The sandbox traffic manager has been
retired.

forks.description: Forks is the specification of each forked entity

labels.description: Labels are used to specify metadata associated with the sandbox as key-value pairs.

localMachineID.description:
Identifier of the machine from where a sandbox containing local workloads
was created or is intended to be ran

resources.description: Resources specifies each required resource to spin up the sandbox

ttl.description:
TTL gives the maximum lifetime of the sandbox.
It may be empty, in which case it defaults to forever.

duration.description:
Duration represents the duration until sandbox end of life.
It should be an unsigned integer not exceeding 32 bits followed by
a units character, which can be one of the following.
  - 'm' for minutes
  - 'h' for hours
  - 'd' for days
  - 'w' for weeks

offsetFrom.description:
OffsetFrom indicates what the Duration is relative to.  It
may be the empty string, "createdAt" or "updatedAt".  The empty string
defaults to meaning "createdAt".

name.description: Name indicates the name of the associated parameter.

value.description:
Value indicates the value passed to the parameter of
the same name.

valueFrom.description: ValueFrom indicates from where to grab the value

match.description:
Matches specifies a disjunction of matching criteria for dispatching
the middleware.  Any empty list means don't dispatch anything.

name.description: Middleware name

workload.description:
Workload specifies a glob which matches
each workload name in the spec.

context.description:
Context contains the LLM messages (system prompt + user context)
that can be fed into any LLM. Only present when mode=context.

explanation.description:
Explanation is a natural-language explanation of why the sandbox
is in its current state and what, if anything, the user can do.
Only present when mode=explain.

mode.description: Mode indicates whether the response contains an LLM explanation or raw context.

status.description: Status is the current machine-readable sandbox status.

name.description: Name of the entity within the Kubernetes cluster.

namespace.description: Namespace within which the entity lives in the Kubernetes cluster.

post.description: Get an authentication code for a device login flow
Get an authentication token for a device login flow
Refresh an authentication token using a refresh token

get.description: Returns the customer-facing OpenAPI (Swagger 2.0) document for
Returns the JSON Schema for plans.PlanSpec, with descriptions
Get organization details
List all artifacts for a given job attempt.

parameters.description: Filter by artifact space, one of 'user' or 'system' (by default there is no filter)
parameters.name: space

List audit events for the org, newest first. Each event carries the authentication method (auth) and, when resolvable, the principal who acted. Time window defaults to the last 24h; event types default to all.

parameters.description: Event type filter; repeatable; defaults to all
parameters.name: type

parameters.description: Filter by principal kind (User|ServiceAccount|System). System selects events with no principal. Matches events that recorded a principal; older events are attributed best-effort in the response but are not matched by this filter.
parameters.name: principalKind

parameters.description: Filter by principal public ID (requires principalKind). Matches events that recorded this principal; older events attributed to it best-effort are not matched.
parameters.name: principalId

parameters.description: Filter by authentication method (jwt|api_key|system); covers all events.
parameters.name: authMethod

get.description: List resource plugins under the specified Signadot org. By default returns only the highest-semver version of each plugin. Pass `?version=all` to expand the response into one row per published (name, version), sorted by name then semver-descending. `?version=latest` is accepted as an explicit alias for the default.

parameters.description: 'latest' (default) returns only the highest-semver version of each plugin; 'all' returns every published version
parameters.name: version

delete.description: Delete a resource plugin version. Omit the version query parameter to delete the latest version. Deletion is blocked when any sandbox references the specific version being deleted.

parameters.description: Resource plugin version (semver); omit for latest
parameters.name: version

get.description: Get the specified resource plugin. Omit the version query parameter to get the latest version.

parameters.description: Resource plugin version (semver); omit for latest
parameters.name: version

put.description: Creates a new version of a resource plugin. The body's `name` field carries the wire form `bareName[@semver]` (e.g. `my-plugin@1.2.0`); its bare part must match the URL path name. A bare-name form (no `@`) publishes the default version `0.0.0`. Versions are immutable; PUTting an existing (name, version) pair returns 409. The version suffix must be a concrete semver; "latest" is only accepted on the read and delete query parameters.

parameters.description: Request to create a resource plugin version
parameters.name: data

get.description: List every published version of the named resource plugin.
get.summary: List versions of a resource plugin

get.description: Returns a sandbox status explanation. In the default mode (mode=explain), generates a natural-language explanation using an LLM fed with cluster context. When mode=context, returns the assembled LLM messages (system prompt + user context) without calling the LLM. Both modes require the sandbox explain AI feature to be enabled, otherwise they return 403 (the assembled context contains sandbox metadata and is itself sensitive).

parameters.description: Response mode: 'explain' (default) calls the LLM, 'context' returns raw LLM messages
parameters.name: mode
